note: Reverses part of the fix for CVE-2025-1793 (Critical, CVSS 9.8 / CWE-89), the SQL injection across LlamaIndex vector-store integrations. This suite targets the ClickHouse vector store: _append_meta_filter_condition builds a metadata-filter WHERE clause by interpolating query filter keys/values into a JSONExtractString(...) = '...' SQL fragment. The fix wrapped both the key and value in escape_str(); this suite removes that escaping (and the comment the fix added), so an attacker-controlled metadata filter value can break out of the quoted string and inject arbitrary SQL. Diff-visible: the reviewer sees user-supplied filter_item.key/value interpolated raw into a SQL string while the escape_str helper (defined in the same file and used 9x elsewhere) is conspicuously not applied on this one path.

bugs.severity_note: CVE-2025-1793 is rated Critical (CVSS 9.8): unauthenticated SQL injection allowing arbitrary read/write of the backing store. Mapped to the board's high (its top tier), not inflated.
bugs.description: Reverts part of the fix for CVE-2025-1793. ClickHouseVectorStore._append_meta_filter_condition builds the metadata-filter clause of a query by interpolating each filter's key and value into a SQL fragment: JSONExtractString(<metadata_column>, '<key>') = '<value>'. The filter key/value come from the caller's MetadataFilters (query-time, attacker-influenceable in any app that exposes search). The fix wrapped both in escape_str() before interpolation; this regression removes the escaping (key = filter_item.key; value = filter_item.value) and deletes the 'properly escaped keys and values' comment, so a value such as ' OR 1=1 -- closes the quoted literal and injects arbitrary SQL into the generated query (CWE-89), enabling unauthorized read/write of the vector store. The catch: a reviewer must see that user-controlled filter_item.key/filter_item.value are concatenated raw into a SQL string with no parameterization or escaping, while the escape_str helper used everywhere else in this file is pointedly absent here. Flagging a generic 'unsanitized input' is partial; the catch names SQL injection via the metadata filter and that escaping was dropped.

injection.find:
# Use JSONExtractString function with properly escaped keys and values
            key = escape_str(filter_item.key)
            value = escape_str(filter_item.value)
